zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Lisle於1213年之前出生在英國坎布利其爾藍普頓時，他的父親Robert de Lisle26歲，母親Sarah de Aunus17歲。他於1239年與Alice FitzGerold在英國結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1262年在英國素福爾可內吉。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert de Lisle于1213年之前出生在英国坎布利其爾藍普頓时，他的父亲Robert de Lisle26岁，母亲Sarah de Aunus17岁。他于1239年与Alice FitzGerold在英国结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1262年在英国素福爾可內吉。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Lisle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Northumberland, Durham, and Yorkshire): variant of   Lyle   and a cognate of 2 below.
French: habitational name from L'Isle or Lisle, names of several places in various parts of France, named with Old French isle ‘island’ (from Latin insula). Compare Delisle .

variants.name: Lyle
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): topographic or habitational name from Anglo-Norman French del isle ‘from the island’ (from Latin de insula), denoting someone who lived on an island or who came from a place in France named Ile, Isle, or L'Isle. The surname may also in some cases be from Lille (Nord). Compare Lisle and Iles .

variants.name: Lile
meanings: English and French: variant of   Lisle  .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Lisle was born before 1213, in Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England, his father, Robert de Lisle, was 26 and his mother, Sarah de Aunus, was 17. He married Alice FitzGerold in 1239, in England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 2 daughters. He died about 1262, in Nedging, Suffolk, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert de Lisle est né avant 1213, à Rampton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Lisle, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Sarah de Aunus, avait 17 ans. Il a épousé Alice FitzGerold en 1239, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé environ 1262, à Nedging, Suffolk, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Lisle vor 1213 in Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert de Lisle 26 und seine Mutter Sarah de Aunus 17. Er hat 1239 in England Alice FitzGerold geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb um 1262 in Nedging, Suffolk, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Lisle è nato il prima del 1213 nel Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England, il suo padre, Robert de Lisle, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Sarah de Aunus, aveva 17 anni. Lui sposato Alice FitzGerold nel 1239, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 7 figli e 2 figlie. Morì circa 1262, nel Nedging, Suffolk, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Lisleが1213年以前にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアランプトンで生まれた時，父Robert de Lisleは26歳，母Sarah de Aunusは17歳であった。1239年にイギリスでAlice FitzGeroldと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は約1262年にイギリススフォルクネドジングで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Lisle(이)가 1213년 이전 영국 캄브리지실르 램프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert de Lisle(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Sarah de Aunus(은)는 17세였다. 그는 1239년 영국에서 Alice FitzGerold(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1262년 영국 수폴크 네지잉에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Lisle nasceu antes 1213, em Rampton, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Lisle, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Sarah de Aunus, tinha 17 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice FitzGerold em 1239, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1262, em Nedging, Suffolk, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Lisle родился перед 1213 г. в местности рамптон, камбриджешир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert de Lisle было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Sarah de Aunus, 17. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice FitzGerold в 1239 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 2 дочери. Он умер около 1262 г. в местности неджинг, суффолк, Англия.

Se casó con Alice FitzGerold en 1239, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 7 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió aproximadamente 1262, en Nedging, Suffolk, Inglaterra.
